Description: Path to the Windows .exe file to run inside the Docker container. Relative to the mounted application directory (e.g., ./Application.exe).
Command line arguments to pass to the application inside the container.
Optional regex pattern to detect when the application inside the container is ready. If empty, the wrapper's own ready state is used.
Docker Windows container image to use. Must be a Windows-based image compatible with process isolation.
Maximum number of CPU cores the container can use (e.g., 1.0 = one core, 0.5 = half a core, 2.0 = two cores).
Maximum memory the container can use. Use Docker format: 512m, 1g, 2g, etc.

Docker network mode for the container. 'nat' provides standard networking behind Windows NAT (uses default route). 'transparent' bridges the container directly to a physical network adapter (container gets its own IP on the physical network, ideal for multi-NIC servers). 'none' disables networking entirely.

EnumValues.nat: NAT - Standard Windows container networking (uses default route)
EnumValues.transparent: Transparent - Bridge to physical adapter (container gets own IP, choose adapter below)

Description: Name of the physical network adapter to bind the container to when using Transparent network mode. Must match the adapter name exactly as shown in Windows (e.g., 'Ethernet 2', 'LAN1'). Run 'Get-NetAdapter' in PowerShell to list available adapters. Ignored when Network Mode is not 'transparent'.

Static IP address to assign to the container when using Transparent network mode. Must be a valid IP on the same subnet as the selected network adapter. If left empty, the container will obtain an IP via DHCP. Ignored when Network Mode is not 'transparent'.

Subnet in CIDR notation for the transparent Docker network (e.g., '192.168.1.0/24'). Required when using a static Container IP Address. Must match the subnet of the selected network adapter. Ignored when Network Mode is not 'transparent' or when Container IP is empty.

Default gateway for the transparent Docker network (e.g., '192.168.1.1'). Required when using a static Container IP Address. Typically the router IP on the selected adapter's subnet. Ignored when Network Mode is not 'transparent' or when Container IP is empty.
Additional volume mounts for the container. One per line in format: host_path:container_path:mode (mode is 'ro' for read-only or 'rw' for read-write). Example: D:\Data:C:\ContainerData:rw

Automatically update Windows root CA certificates from Windows Update when the container starts. Enable this if your application connects to HTTPS endpoints (e.g., Discord, APIs) and you see SSL certificate verification errors. Adds ~3-5 seconds to container startup.

EnumValues.true: Enabled - Update root CA certificates on each start (recommended for HTTPS apps)
